title: haplopappus carthamoides var. erythropappus
intro: Haplopappus carthamoides var. erythropappus, also known as the Red Stemmed Haplopappus, is a perennial plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to the western United States. The medicinal properties of this plant include its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids. Haplopappus carthamoides var. erythropappus is valued for its health benefits, including its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the body. Traditionally, herbal preparations of this plant are made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to treat a variety of ailments, including digestive issues, fever, and skin problems.
